nodes.label: AI as code generation assistant
nodes.label_negative: AI as code generator for ambiguous requirements
nodes.grounding_span: I typically use AI to write Julia code with well understood requirements

nodes.label: AI as strategic thinking partner
nodes.grounding_span: I use AI to analyze parts of the codebase and ask it to provide architectural and strategic insights

nodes.label: AI's tendency to fill in blanks without clarification
nodes.label_negative: AI's tendency to ask clarifying questions before generating
nodes.grounding_span: What I often see if the AI is eager to 'fill in the blanks' instead of pausing to ask for clarification

nodes.label: AI feedback as useful but fallible
nodes.grounding_span: My concern is the AI isn't bold or confident enough to strike down actual bad ideas

nodes.label: AI's understanding of project-specific context
nodes.grounding_span: The AI seems much more intelligent with general world knowledge, than specifically about my code base

nodes.label: AI as project persistence enabler
nodes.label_negative: AI's inability to maintain persistent project memory
nodes.grounding_span: better tools in my case means automatically maintaining memory about a project

nodes.label: Reading knowledge of coding languages via AI
nodes.label_negative: AI's inability to understand semantics of code elements
nodes.grounding_span: wrote overly complicated graph code (not understanding semantics of nodes in the graph)

nodes.label: AI as instant subject matter expert
nodes.label_negative: AI as replacement for deep learning
nodes.grounding_span: with AI we can come up with expert-at-anything with pretty short call-up time

nodes.label: Productivity and efficiency
nodes.grounding_span: wrote RPC package all by itself with fully working tests

nodes.label: Strategic decision-making and ROI evaluation
nodes.grounding_span: aiming to maximize business value

nodes.label: Understanding and control over codebase
nodes.grounding_span: lacks intuition about what's most relevant about a particular piece of code

nodes.label: Critical thinking and skill development
nodes.grounding_span: force me to think through the problem more thoroughly

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI as a helpful tool
nodes.grounding_span: most satisfied: wrote RPC package all by itself with fully working tests

nodes.label: Frustration with AI errors
nodes.grounding_span: It would be nice if there was an interview process from the AI before it generates large amounts of code

nodes.label: Negative stance toward AI for sincere communication
nodes.grounding_span: My concern is the AI isn't bold or confident enough to strike down actual bad ideas

nodes.label: Frustration with AI's lack of novelty in design
nodes.grounding_span: it's very rare that the AI understands 'what's important here'

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for unit testing
nodes.grounding_span: pretty happy: wrote tests for exist code while discovering bugs

nodes.label: Frustration with AI's semantic misunderstanding in graph code
nodes.grounding_span: frustrated: wrote overly complicated graph code (not understanding semantics of nodes in the graph)

nodes.label: Acceptance of AI-driven shift from deep learning to on-demand competence
nodes.grounding_span: it has deprioritized learning skills such as SQL...with AI we can come up with expert-at-anything

nodes.label: Iterative refinement through conversation
nodes.grounding_span: it usually ends up being an iterative process, where an ongoing conversation provides the full details

nodes.label: Strategic prioritization of learning investment
nodes.grounding_span: it has deprioritized learning skills such as SQL...with AI we can come up with expert-at-anything
